review_basis.source: Google Cloud IAM roles and permissions

scope_classes.project_setup: Permission remains project-level in thesis-demo-v1 because it creates service accounts, custom roles, project IAM bindings, or enables services.
scope_classes.resource_candidate: Permission can likely be narrowed by separating bootstrap identity from deployment identity or by moving to resource-level bindings after live validation.
scope_classes.runtime_package: Permission supports Cloud Functions source/package upload and generated runtime resources.
scope_classes.known_gap: Permission belongs to an explicit thesis-demo-v1 gap and should move to a bootstrap-only identity in least-privilege-v2.

permission_groups.name: setup
permission_groups.reason: Project IAM binding, service account, custom role, and API enablement are still part of thesis-demo-v1 deployment setup.

permission_groups.name: storage
permission_groups.reason: Cloud Storage is used for function source packages plus cold/archive storage.

permission_groups.name: pubsub_eventarc
permission_groups.reason: Pub/Sub and Eventarc support L1 ingestion and function triggers.

permission_groups.name: functions_run_build
permission_groups.reason: Cloud Functions Gen2 provisions Cloud Run services and uses Cloud Build for builds.

permission_groups.name: workflows
permission_groups.reason: Terraform creates google_workflows_workflow for optional event notification workflows.

permission_groups.name: firestore_scheduler
permission_groups.reason: Firestore and Cloud Scheduler support hot storage plus mover jobs.

known_gaps: resourcemanager.projects.setIamPolicy is needed by google_project_iam_member in thesis-demo-v1 but should move to bootstrap-only setup in least-privilege-v2.
serviceusage.services.enable/disable and iam.roles.* are setup permissions and should not remain on the runtime deployment identity after the bootstrap/deployment split is finalized.
Project creation for organization-account mode remains outside the project-level custom role and requires bootstrap/admin credentials.
E2E deployment verification is intentionally deferred until the larger refactoring roadmap reaches an end-to-end testable state.
next_validation: Run gcloud iam list-testable-permissions for the active project and compare includedPermissions support levels.
Run supervised GCP deployment/destroy smoke with thesis-demo-v1 credentials.
Capture IAM/API failures to produce least-privilege-v2 with bootstrap-only setup permissions removed from runtime credentials.
